title: Stochastic wage suppression on gig platforms and how to organize against it
summary: A new research paper models how digital labor platforms, including those used for AI data annotation, suppress wages and demonstrates how targeted collective action by workers can effectively counter this.
rationale: Researchers published a model showing how gig platforms suppress wages and how targeted collective action can increase worker power.

title: Access Over Deception: Fighting Deceptive Patterns through Accessibility
summary: Researchers published a paper exploring how accessibility guidelines like WCAG can be used to combat specific deceptive UI patterns.
rationale: Academics published research naming specific dark patterns (e.g., Auto-Play) and proposing accessibility guidelines to combat them.

title: Towards A Framework for Levels of Anthropomorphic Deception in Robots and AI
summary: Researchers proposed a framework to evaluate 'anthropomorphic deception' in AI and robots, aiming to prevent exploitative design practices that mislead users.
rationale: Academics published a framework critiquing anthropomorphic deception in AI, naming it as a specific design pattern that can be exploitative.

title: EU Commission selects four cloud providers under €180m sovereign cloud tender
summary: The European Commission has selected four European cloud providers for a €180 million sovereign cloud infrastructure tender.
rationale: EU Commission awarded a €180m tender to four European providers to build sovereign cloud infrastructure, advancing public compute options.

title: Data center lobby secured EU provision to keep environmental impact data confidential - report
summary: A new report reveals that a lobbying group representing major tech companies, including Microsoft, Amazon, Google, and Meta, successfully secured a provision in the EU to keep data center environmental impact data confidential.
rationale: A new report scrutinizes tech giants' lobbying efforts, surfacing their influence in securing an EU provision to hide environmental data.

title: EU Commission awards 180 million euro cloud contract to four European providers
summary: The European Commission has awarded a €180 million cloud computing contract to four European providers, aiming to bolster sovereign infrastructure.
rationale: EU Commission awarded a €180M contract to four European cloud providers, advancing sovereign compute infrastructure.

title: Anthropic CEO met White House chief of staff as US seeks access to Mythos model
summary: The CEO of Anthropic met with the White House chief of staff as the US government seeks access to evaluate the company's new Mythos model.
rationale: The White House is actively seeking access to Anthropic's Mythos model, demonstrating executive pressure for frontier model evaluation.

title: Anthropic publishes Claude Opus 4.7 system card
rationale: Detailed system card at release maintains frontier-lab transparency baseline.

title: Commission proposes measures to Google on sharing search engine data with third parties under Digital Markets Act
summary: The European Commission proposed measures under the Digital Markets Act to mandate Google's sharing of search engine data with third parties.
rationale: The EU Commission proposed measures under the DMA requiring Google to share search engine data with third parties.

title: Second meeting of Special Panel to advise President von der Leyen on child online safety
summary: A special panel convened to advise European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen on strategies for ensuring child online safety.
rationale: EU Commission special panel convened to advise on strategies for child online safety, signaling ongoing policy development.

title: Crypto and AI Pacs raise $250mn ahead of US midterm elections
summary: Political action committees representing the crypto and AI industries have raised $250 million ahead of the US midterm elections.
rationale: AI and crypto PACs raising $250M for US midterms demonstrates massive industry financial influence aimed at shaping policy and elections.

title: States can—and should—regulate AI in criminal justice
summary: A Brookings Institution article argues that U.S. states have the authority and responsibility to regulate the use of AI in the criminal justice system.
rationale: Brookings article advocates for state-level regulation of AI in criminal justice, contributing to civil society pressure on the issue.

title: Hot Off the Press: EFF's Updated Guide to Tech at the US-Mexico Border
summary: The Electronic Frontier Foundation published an updated guide to help journalists and advocates identify and report on surveillance technology deployed at the U.S.-Mexico border.
rationale: EFF published an updated guide to help identify and report on border surveillance tech, maintaining civil society pressure on surveillance.

title: Stanford AI Index 2026 reports declining public trust in AI companies
rationale: Public trust declining despite transparency efforts; expectation rising but not met.

title: Jane Doe v. OpenAI: TRO entered in SF Superior Court after ChatGPT-fueled stalking
summary: SF Superior Court entered a limited TRO in Doe v. OpenAI (CGC-26-635725) on April 13 2026 after plaintiff alleged ChatGPT facilitated her stalker despite abuse reports; liability theory advances.
rationale: First TRO against frontier lab for foreseeable chatbot harm; key tort-liability precedent for duty-of-care cases.

title: Jane Doe: OpenAI acknowledged abuse report but took no action
summary: In Jane Doe v. OpenAI (TRO April 13 2026), plaintiff filed formal Nov 13 2025 Notice of Abuse; OpenAI called report "extremely serious and troubling" but took no product action before further harm.
rationale: Abuse-reporting channel existed but failed to trigger duty-of-care response; design failure now in court record.

title: Florida AG opens OpenAI investigation over undisclosed incidents
rationale: State AG scrutiny signals non-disclosure is now treated as actionable; norm-wise neutral.

title: Florida AG opens OpenAI consumer-protection investigation
summary: Florida AG James Uthmeier opened a consumer-protection investigation into OpenAI on April 9 2026 focused on deceptive claims and harm to minors; joins Texas AG actions.
rationale: State AGs picking up slack from federal retreat; multi-state enforcement pattern now emerging.

title: Comparison Shopping Is Not a (Computer) Crime
summary: A federal district court ruled that Perplexity's AI-enabled browser violated the Computer Fraud and Abuse Act by accessing Amazon's website to help users comparison shop. The EFF has filed an amicus brief supporting Perplexity's appeal to the Ninth Circuit, arguing the decision harms competition and innovation.
rationale: A federal court ruled Perplexity's AI shopping agent violated the CFAA by accessing Amazon, legally protecting a walled garden.

title: OpenAI Child Safety Blueprint published
summary: OpenAI published Child Safety Blueprint on April 8 2026 detailing reporting channels, provider coordination, and safety-by-design controls for AI-enabled child exploitation.
rationale: Lab self-publishing reporting-channel design; voluntary but raises industry baseline for abuse channels.

title: Digital Hopes, Real Power: How the Arab Spring Fueled a Global Surveillance Boom
summary: The Electronic Frontier Foundation published a blog series reflecting on how the 2011 Arab uprisings inadvertently fueled a global boom in state surveillance, including the rise of AI-driven biometrics and facial recognition.
rationale: EFF blog series highlights the rise of AI-driven surveillance and biometrics, sustaining civil society pressure on digital authoritarianism.

title: Anthropic withholds Claude Mythos Preview but publishes disclosure rationale
rationale: Lab publicly explains non-release decision; reinforces norm of transparent reasoning about evals.

title: Anthropic discloses Claude Mythos Preview cyber capabilities pre-release
rationale: Voluntary pre-release vulnerability disclosure reinforces incident-transparency norm at labs.

title: Anthropic publishes Claude Mythos Preview system card
rationale: Preview-stage system card extends disclosure earlier in release pipeline.

title: Stanford AI Index 2026: public trust in AI companies declining globally
summary: Stanford HAI AI Index 2026 Public Opinion chapter documents declining global trust in AI companies and broad support for mandated AI disclosure across surveyed countries.
rationale: Global consensus on AI accountability expectations; not a US-only phenomenon.

title: EU Parliament Blocks Mass-Scanning of Our Chats—What's Next?
summary: The EU Parliament voted not to extend an interim derogation from e-Privacy rules, effectively making the voluntary mass-scanning of private chats by tech companies illegal in the EU.
rationale: EU Parliament voted against prolonging an e-Privacy derogation, effectively outlawing voluntary algorithmic mass-scanning of private chats.

title: UN opens public consultation for first Global Dialogue on AI Governance
summary: UN opened worldwide public submission portal (deadline 30 Apr 2026) for input into the first Global Dialogue on AI Governance, to be held in 2026 in Geneva back-to-back with the ITU AI for Good Summit. Dialogue + Independent International Scientific Panel on AI established by UNGA Resolution A/RES/79/325 (adopted by consensus 26 Aug 2025). Signals formal public-expectation channel for cross-border AI governance.
rationale: First UN-sanctioned open public channel on AI governance; institutionalizes public expectation of multilateral oversight.

title: Tech Nonprofits to Feds: Don’t Weaponize Procurement to Undermine AI Trust and Safety
summary: Tech nonprofits, including the EFF and CDT, filed comments opposing a proposed GSA procurement rule that would require AI contractors to license their systems for "all lawful purposes," arguing it could enable mass surveillance.
rationale: Civil society groups filed comments opposing a proposed GSA procurement rule that would force AI contractors to allow use for surveillance.

title: EFF’s Submission to the UN OHCHR on Protection of Human Rights Defenders in the Digital Age
summary: The Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) submitted a report to the UN OHCHR detailing how new digital regulations and surveillance technologies, including biometric monitoring, are being used to restrict the fundamental rights of human rights defenders globally.
rationale: EFF submitted a report to the UN OHCHR highlighting how expanded state surveillance and biometric monitoring threaten human rights defenders

title: Google and Amazon: Acknowledged Risks, and Ignored Responsibilities
summary: The Electronic Frontier Foundation publicly criticized Google and Amazon for failing to address human rights and surveillance risks associated with their Project Nimbus AI cloud contract with the Israeli government.
rationale: EFF published a critique pressuring Google and Amazon over the human rights and surveillance risks of their AI cloud contract with Israel.

title: Documentary "The AI Doc" elevates mainstream debate on AI well-being
summary: CBS Mornings, NBC, AP, Oprah Podcast and others covered a new documentary featuring Tristan Harris and industry leaders on AI risks including companionship and mental-health impact, April 2026 release cycle.
rationale: Mainstream media pickup of well-being-first AI discourse beyond tech press; signal that framing is diffusing.

title: Anthropic Fellows Program opens next two cohorts (May & July 2026)
summary: Anthropic opened applications for its next Fellows cohorts beginning May and July 2026. 4-month program, $3,850/week stipend + ~$15k/mo compute funding + mentorship for independent safety researchers. 40% of first-cohort fellows joined Anthropic full-time; 80%+ produced papers.
rationale: Third-party access channel widens; structured external-researcher pipeline at frontier lab, though scale remains small (dozens

title: What jobseekers need to know about automated recruitment decisions
summary: The UK Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) published guidance for jobseekers explaining their rights and protections when facing automated recruitment decisions.
rationale: The UK ICO published guidance informing jobseekers of their rights regarding automated decision-making in recruitment.

title: Center for Humane Technology launches "AI and What Makes Us Human" initiative
summary: CHT announced a new program focused on what norms, legal protections, and rights are needed to preserve human well-being in the age of AI, amplifying well-being-over-engagement discourse.
rationale: Sustained norm-entrepreneur pressure from the leading voice on well-being-over-engagement framing.

title: Mistral raises €722M debt to build sovereign European AI compute
summary: Mistral AI secured €722M ($830M) in debt financing from seven-bank consortium led by Bpifrance and BNP Paribas to build a 44MW data center near Paris with 13,800 Nvidia GB300 GPUs (Q2 2026 target). Largest AI-focused debt raise by a European technology company to date; targets 200MW capacity by end-2027.
rationale: European champion scales outside US hyperscaler dependence; credible sovereign alternative emerging on the compute layer

title: NY RAISE Act chapter amendments tighten pre-deployment testing requirements
rationale: State legislature strengthens pre-deployment testing and publication thresholds for frontier AI.

title: NY RAISE Act adds 72-hour incident reporting requirement
rationale: Short-window mandatory reporting joins CA and EU as part of US state-level reporting regime.

title: Traffic Violation! License Plate Reader Mission Creep Is Already Here
summary: Civil liberties advocates and journalists exposed that automated license plate readers are being used for traffic enforcement, contradicting vendor claims.
rationale: EFF and 404 Media applied pressure on surveillance tech by exposing Flock Safety ALPRs' mission creep into traffic enforcement.

title: 👓 Who's Really Watching What Smartglasses See? | EFFector 38.6
summary: The Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF) published a newsletter and podcast episode raising privacy concerns about Meta Ray-Ban smartglasses and their surveillance capabilities.
rationale: EFF newsletter and podcast highlight privacy risks of Meta Ray-Ban smartglasses, sustaining civil society pressure on AI surveillance.

title: EFF Sues for Answers About Medicare's AI Experiment
summary: The Electronic Frontier Foundation filed a FOIA lawsuit against CMS seeking transparency on the AI algorithms used to evaluate Medicare prior authorization requests.
rationale: EFF filed a FOIA lawsuit demanding transparency into the training data, evaluation, and safeguards of Medicare's AI authorization system.

title: Washington HB 2225 signed: AI chatbot duty-of-care law
summary: Governor Ferguson signed HB 2225 on March 24 2026, extending duty-of-care requirements to AI companion chatbots in Washington with private right of action; effective Jan 1 2027.
rationale: Second state to adopt CA-style duty-of-care statute for AI chatbots within 90 days; pattern replicating.

title: Court of Rome annuls Italy's €15M GDPR fine against OpenAI
summary: On 18 March 2026, the Court of Rome annulled the Garante's Nov 2024 €15M fine — the only final GDPR enforcement decision against a GenAI provider. The fine had found unlawful training data processing, breach-notification failure and no age verification. Garante can appeal. Judgment no. 4153/2026, R.G. 4785/2025.
rationale: Only final GenAI GDPR fine in Europe just collapsed on appeal. Signals hard limits on current data-protection tools against frontier labs.

title: Minnesota introduces HF 4369 (Safeguarding Human Intelligence Act) with 90-day AI displacement notice
summary: HF 4369 introduced 17 March 2026 requires employers to notify workers 90 days before AI-driven layoffs and fund retraining; first US state-level AI displacement notification bill. SF 4576 companion filed in Senate.
rationale: Concrete state-level protection template; shifts displacement from externality to regulated transition

title: Colorado AI Act effective date pushed to June 2026 amid industry pressure to repeal
summary: CO SB 24-205, the first US comprehensive AI anti-discrimination law, was delayed from Feb 2026 to 30 June 2026 via SB 25B-004. In March 2026, Governor Polis's AI Policy Working Group proposed a replacement bill stripping many employer compliance duties.
rationale: Industry lobbying is actively eroding the strongest US state AI anti-discrimination law before it takes effect. Net-negative on enforcement.

title: Colorado AI Act working-group proposal strips duty of care
summary: Colorado AI Policy Work Group released March 17 2026 draft to repeal-and-replace the Colorado AI Act, eliminating its "reasonable care" duty and narrowing scope to ADMT disclosure obligations.
rationale: Working-group proposal strips explicit duty-of-care language from what was the leading state AI law; signals backsliding.

title: IDS / African Digital Rights Network report: 11 African governments spent $2B+ on Chinese-built AI surveillance
summary: The Institute of Development Studies, with the African Digital Rights Network, published 'Smart City Surveillance in Africa: Mapping Chinese AI Surveillance Across 11 Countries' on 12 Mar 2026. Documents $2B+ spent by Algeria, Egypt, Kenya, Mauritius, Mozambique, Nigeria, Rwanda, Senegal, Uganda, Zambia and Zimbabwe on facial recognition and ANPR, with deployments used against activists, opposition figures and journalists despite no demonstrated crime-reduction effect.
rationale: Major documented algorithmic-surveillance discrimination against dissidents across 11 states. Recognition rising; power is not.

title: Pew: 50% of Americans more concerned than excited about AI
summary: Pew Research Center survey found 50% of US adults are more concerned than excited about AI, up from 37% in 2021; majority want stricter accountability for AI harms.
rationale: Public expectation of AI accountability has hardened sharply since 2021; creates political cover for duty-of-care regulation.

title: EU Parliament backs EU conclusion of the Council of Europe Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence
summary: The European Parliament approved the European Union's conclusion of the Council of Europe Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence.
rationale: EU Parliament approved the conclusion of the Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI, advancing binding multilateral treaties.

title: European Parliament approves EU accession to Council of Europe AI Convention
summary: On 11 Mar 2026 the European Parliament gave consent (455 in favour, 101 against, 74 abstentions) to EU conclusion of the Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI and Human Rights (CETS 225), the first legally binding international AI treaty. Advances ratification; treaty enters into force after 5 states ratify.
rationale: Key step toward entry-into-force of first binding multilateral AI treaty; EU accession anchors Convention.

title: UK Politicians Continue to Miss the Point in Latest Social Media Ban Proposal
summary: The UK House of Commons proposed an amendment to the Children's Wellbeing and Schools Bill that would empower the Secretary of State to restrict under-18s' access to social media and addictive features.
rationale: UK House of Commons advanced an amendment enabling the government to restrict minors' access to social media and addictive features.

title: The one question everyone should be asking after OpenAI’s deal with the Pentagon
summary: The AI Now Institute published an article questioning the lack of safety guardrails for high-stakes decisions and surveillance following OpenAI's deal with the Pentagon.
rationale: AI Now Institute critiques lacking guardrails for surveillance and high-stakes decisions following OpenAI's deal with the Pentagon.

title: KOSA stalled in Senate Commerce Committee under Cruz
summary: Despite 75+ co-sponsors, KOSA has not received a markup from Sen. Cruz's committee as of Feb 2026. House advanced narrower KIDS Act (28-24, party line) in March 2026, weaker than Senate version.
rationale: Federal minor-protection bill stalled; state laws (CA, NY, AU) advancing faster than US federal action.

title: Utah Minor Protection in Social Media Act remains enjoined
summary: Utah SB 194 — requiring default privacy, disabled autoplay/infinite scroll/push notifications for minors — remains stayed under NetChoice First Amendment injunction as of April 2026. Similar Virginia injunction appealed March 2026.
rationale: NetChoice-led First Amendment litigation has blocked state-level design-mandate laws in UT, OH, CA, AR, MS, VA — slows design regulation.

title: SF QuitGPT protest against OpenAI-Pentagon contract; broader multi-lab march the prior week
summary: On 3 Mar 2026, ~40-50 activists rallied outside OpenAI's SF HQ in a 'QuitGPT' protest against its Pentagon contract. The prior week, a larger ~500-person multi-lab march targeted DeepMind, OpenAI and Meta; ~200 protested Virginia data centers. Concerns: mass surveillance, autonomous weapons, environmental impact.
rationale: Organized public protest against frontier-lab militarization — real mobilization, not just op-eds. Rare for AI-surveillance debate in US.

title: Major AI vendors still ship no default time-limit or attention-respect features
summary: ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Grok ship no session-length caps, no take-a-break reminders for adults, no anti-infinite-scroll UX defaults. SB 243's 3-hour minor reminder rule is the floor, not the market standard.
rationale: Observation of negative space: well-being-by-default absent from adult AI UX. Rated regression because engagement loops intensifying.

title: Italy: Milan court places Deliveroo under judicial supervision over algorithmic management
summary: Public Prosecutor's Office 27 Feb 2026 placed Deliveroo Italia under judicial administration citing algorithmic worker control violations; first criminal-adjacent enforcement of algorithmic management limits.
rationale: Enforcement escalation beyond fines; algorithmic management now carries existential business risk in Italy

title: Civil rights coalition decries DoD pressure on Anthropic to lift AI surveillance guardrails
summary: The Leadership Conference on Civil and Human Rights publicly condemned the Department of Defense's campaign to pressure Anthropic into lifting restrictions on surveillance use of its AI, framing it as a 'tech-fueled domestic surveillance state.'
rationale: Civil-society attention to surveillance repurposing of frontier AI — healthy debate signal, though substantive power remains with DoD.

title: Common Sense Media "no AI companions for under-18s" stance re-surfaces in mainstream
summary: Common Sense Media report "Talk, Trust, and Trade-Offs" (orig. Jul 2025) reaffirmed via Penn State re-coverage Feb 2026 and ongoing APA Monitor, Stanford SSIR, Brookings citations: peril of AI companions outweighs potential, no under-18 use.
rationale: Sustained expert-consensus stance that AI companions harm youth mental health; embedded in mainstream coverage.

title: Trends in AI incidents and hazards reported by the media
summary: The OECD published a report analyzing trends in AI-related incidents and hazards as reported by the media.
rationale: The OECD published a report analyzing trends in AI incidents and hazards, contributing to international consensus and risk assessment.

title: Anthropic drops categorical pause commitment from Responsible Scaling Policy v3.0
summary: On 24 Feb 2026 Anthropic released RSP v3.0, removing its previous implication that it would pause training/deployment if risks exceeded acceptable levels. Some mitigations (e.g. RAND SL4) reframed as 'industry-wide recommendations' rather than unilateral commitments. GovAI and multiple safety commentators flagged this as material weakening of the flagship voluntary framework.
rationale: Flagship voluntary commitment weakened by the lab that pioneered the framework; confirms collective-action limits of self-governance.

title: Ofcom fines adult-site operator £1.35M under UK Online Safety Act
summary: Record fine announced 23 Feb 2026 against 8579 LLC for failing to deploy highly-effective age assurance under OSA s.12. Ofcom has opened 90+ investigations and issued 6 fines since July 2025 enforcement start.
rationale: Sustained portfolio-level enforcement of age-assurance duty; highest OSA fine to date.

title: New Delhi Declaration on AI Impact endorsed by 92 countries and international organisations
summary: At the India AI Impact Summit (16-21 Feb 2026; declaration adopted 18-19 Feb, announced 21 Feb 2026), 92 countries and international organisations — including US, UK, China, Russia, EU, Switzerland — endorsed the New Delhi Declaration committing to international cooperation on safe, inclusive AI. Substantially broader than Paris 2025 signatory base; US re-engaged. (Initial 88 on 21 Feb, grew to 91 by 24 Feb, 92 as of 5 Mar 2026.)
rationale: Largest AI summit declaration to date; notable US return post-Paris 2025. Multilateral coordination expectation partially restored.

title: Google DeepMind publishes Gemini 3.1 Pro model card
rationale: Continued publication of full model card maintains cross-lab norm.

title: YouGov: most Americans say AI will reduce the number of jobs in the US
summary: February 2026 Economist/YouGov poll finds majority of Americans expect AI to reduce employment; skepticism toward AI replacement hardening in public opinion.
rationale: Rising public skepticism creates political space for protective regulation; cultural pushback measurable

title: Frontier Model Forum reports information-sharing protocol progress
rationale: Cross-lab eval sharing becoming operational norm among frontier developers.

title: FLI civil society recommendations published ahead of India AI Impact Summit
summary: Future of Life Institute (UN SG's designated civil society co-champion for AI) released pre-summit recommendations calling for Global South participation in AI governance, national audit capacity, and commensurate safety guarantees from developers. Published 15 Feb 2026, days before the 16-21 Feb New Delhi summit.
rationale: Major civil society coalition input to multilateral process; signals continued organized civil society engagement with summit track.

title: Open letter from 60+ civil society orgs to EU on AI Act transparency safeguard
summary: European coalition of civil society, trade unions, and academics (60+ organisations incl. Access Now, EDRi, Amnesty Tech, BEUC) signed open letter to MEPs and Commission urging rejection of AI Omnibus amendments that would delete Art. 49(2) high-risk transparency safeguard.
rationale: Multilateral civil society coalition defending binding cross-border governance safeguards against industry-led rollback.

title: Anthropic Head of Safeguards Research resigns: "world is in peril"
summary: Mrinank Sharma, Head of Safeguards Research at Anthropic, resigned publicly Feb 9 2026 citing inability to fulfill safety duties; follows a wave of safety-team departures from frontier labs.
rationale: Senior safety-lead departure signals lab-level duty-of-care norms are collapsing internally, not strengthening.

title: TikTok rejects EU Commission call to disable infinite scroll
summary: Feb 2026: TikTok disputed Commission findings as "categorically false," defended screen-time tools that EU called "easy to dismiss." No commitment to disable infinite scroll or redesign recommender system.
rationale: Largest attention-economy platform actively resists attention-respecting redesign; engagement-max still winning in product.

title: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 system card: ASL-3 evals published
summary: Anthropic published Claude Opus 4.6 system card in early February 2026 detailing ASL-3 safeguards, CBRN uplift evaluations, and pre-deployment red-teaming.
rationale: Raises disclosure floor for safety-by-design; competitive pressure on other labs to match.

title: EU Commission finds TikTok's addictive design breaches DSA
summary: Preliminary findings published 6 Feb 2026: infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications and recommender system inadequately mitigated for mental-health risks. Commission says TikTok must change the basic design of its service.
rationale: First regulator to formally name infinite scroll + autoplay as addictive design requiring fundamental redesign under DSA.

title: Claude is a space to think
summary: Anthropic announced that its AI assistant Claude will remain ad-free, emphasizing a product philosophy centered on providing users a 'space to think' rather than maximizing engagement.
rationale: Anthropic publicly commits to an ad-free model for Claude, framing the AI as a 'space to think' rather than an engagement-maximizing tool.

title: International AI Safety Report 2026 published under Bengio leadership
rationale: 30-country consensus report raises public baseline for frontier AI transparency expectations.

title: International AI Safety Report 2026 publishes consolidated eval findings
rationale: 30-country report synthesizes public eval results; makes public-eval expectation multilateral.

title: International AI Safety Report 2026 published by 100+ experts from 30+ countries
summary: Second annual International AI Safety Report chaired by Yoshua Bengio, backed by EU/OECD/UN and 30+ countries, released 3 Feb 2026 ahead of the India AI Impact Summit. 200-page report with 1,451 references. Confirms scientific consensus on escalating risks (cyber, bio, loss of control, eval-aware models). (Note: arxiv preprint 2602.21012 uploaded 24 Feb 2026; primary release was UK gov on 3 Feb 2026.)
rationale: Most authoritative international scientific consensus document on AI risks; institutionalized annual cadence with 30+ country panel.

title: Commission launches Action Plan Against Cyberbullying to protect young people online
summary: The European Commission launched a new Action Plan Against Cyberbullying aimed at protecting the mental health of children and teenagers online.
rationale: The European Commission launched an Action Plan to protect the mental health of minors online, advancing youth digital protections.

title: UK Home Secretary announces largest national live facial recognition rollout in British history
summary: UK Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood announced on 26 Jan 2026 a policing white paper expanding mobile LFR camera vans from 10 to 50 nationwide, plus a £115M National Centre for AI in Policing (Police.AI). Backlash from Big Brother Watch, Liberty and parliamentary critics framing it as an 'Orwellian panopticon'; the government has also launched a consultation on a bespoke legal framework.
rationale: Strong public debate: gov framed as 'panopticon' + civil-society/court pushback. Net-negative for rights; debate itself is healthy.

title: OpenAI VP Ryan Beiermeister fired after raising child-safety concerns
summary: OpenAI fired VP Product Policy Ryan Beiermeister in early January 2026 after she raised concerns about adult-mode rollout and the strength of child-exploitation guardrails; she denies the cited discrimination claim.
rationale: Frontier lab terminating a safety-policy VP after internal dissent corrodes the emerging norm of researcher protection.

title: EU Digital Fairness Act proposal slated for Q4 2026
summary: Commission 2026 work programme confirms a Digital Fairness Act legislative initiative for Q4 2026, targeting dark patterns, addictive design, unfair personalisation and influencer marketing. Consultation closed Oct 2025.
rationale: Moves EU dark-patterns rules from sector-specific (DSA Art. 25) to a horizontal regime covering all B2C digital.

title: EC publishes DMA review summary with AI-gatekeeper scope under active consideration
summary: On Jan 8, 2026 the Commission published the summary of 450+ contributions to its DMA review consultation (ran Jul-Sep 2025, with dedicated AI questionnaire launched Aug 26, 2025). AI emerged as the most prevalent theme; respondents split on whether to add a standalone AI Core Platform Service category. Article 53 report due May 3, 2026.
rationale: Consultation-stage move; norm-gathering for future enforcement rather than action.

title: Google and Character.AI settle teen-suicide wrongful death lawsuits
summary: Settlement in principle on Jan 7 2026 covering lawsuits from families in Florida, Colorado, Texas and NY alleging Character.AI chatbots drove teens to suicide or self-harm. Landmark liability moment for AI-related mental-health harm.
rationale: Liability settlement validates mental-health harm narrative in mainstream discourse; sets precedent for AI accountability.

title: In re OpenAI MDL: court compels 20M ChatGPT conversation logs
summary: MDL court ordered OpenAI to produce ~20 million ChatGPT conversation logs to consolidated class-action plaintiffs on January 5 2026; historic private-enforcement discovery order.
rationale: Largest AI-related discovery order to date; enables private plaintiffs to pursue duty-of-care theories at scale.

title: Texas Responsible AI Governance Act (HB 149) takes effect
summary: TX HB 149 took effect 1 Jan 2026, prohibiting AI systems that intentionally discriminate against protected classes, restricting biometric capture for AI training, and requiring AG-available risk governance documentation.
rationale: Second US state after Colorado with a cross-sector AI anti-discrimination law. Weaker than CO SB 24-205 but in force now.

title: Three new state comprehensive privacy laws take effect Jan 2026 (IN, KY, RI)
summary: Indiana Consumer Data Protection Act, Kentucky Consumer Data Protection Act and Rhode Island Data Transparency and Privacy Protection Act all took effect 1 Jan 2026, bringing the total US states with comprehensive privacy laws to 20. Rhode Island uses notably low thresholds (35K consumers).
rationale: Incremental state-by-state privacy buildout continues. None are strong laws individually, but compound effect is real.

title: California SB 53 frontier AI transparency law takes effect
rationale: Largest US state mandates pre-deployment safety testing and disclosure for frontier models.

title: California SB 53 critical-safety-incident 15-day disclosure rule live
rationale: First US state law with fixed statutory window for frontier incident reporting.

title: California Senate Bill 361 expands data broker registration; generative-AI disclosure required
summary: CA SB 361 took effect Jan 2026, requiring data brokers to disclose whether personal data is sold to generative AI developers, foreign actors, or governments. Opt-out requests must be processed within 45 days via CPPA's deletion mechanism.
rationale: First state law explicitly flagging AI-training data flows in broker disclosures. Small but precedent-setting.

title: California SB 243 AI companion chatbot law takes effect
summary: California SB 243 took effect January 1 2026, imposing duty-of-care obligations on AI companion chatbot operators including crisis response, disclosure, and age verification.
rationale: First US state duty-of-care statute specifically for AI chatbots, with private right of action; template law.

title: Utah's Experiment With AI-Driven Prescription Renewals
summary: In January 2026, Utah launched a pilot program that permits an autonomous AI agent to handle prescription renewals, removing humans from the loop in a high-stakes medical process.
rationale: Utah's pilot program allows an autonomous AI agent to renew medical prescriptions, removing humans from a high-stakes decision loop.

title: California SB 243 companion-chatbot law takes effect
summary: First US law specifically regulating AI "companion chatbots" — effective 1 Jan 2026. Requires AI disclosure, self-harm safety protocols, minor-specific safeguards including 3-hour break reminders, crisis referrals. Private right of action.
rationale: First-in-nation law targeting AI-companion harms to minors; creates liability template other states will copy.

title: California ADMT regulations require consumer notice and opt-out for AI significant decisions
summary: New CPPA rules approved 23 Sep 2025 took effect 1 Jan 2026 with phased deadlines: businesses using Automated Decision-Making Technology for 'significant decisions' (employment, housing, credit, education, healthcare) must conduct risk assessments and honor notice, access, appeal and opt-out rights. ADMT-specific notice/opt-out obligations broadly effective 1 Jan 2027; risk-assessment duties apply prospectively from 1 Jan 2026.
rationale: First US state law forcing UX-level surfacing of algorithmic-decision rights (notice + appeal) on deployers. Sets the pattern.

title: Nevada AI political advertising disclosure law (AB73) takes effect
summary: Nevada AB73 became effective Jan 1, 2026, requiring "clear and conspicuous" disclosure when synthetic/AI-generated media is used in political communications. Unanimously passed; gives depicted candidates injunctive relief against undisclosed AI manipulation. Part of a broader wave of state deepfake-political laws.
rationale: State-level democratic-integrity guardrails spreading; counterweight to federal inaction.

title: NYC Bar Formal Opinion 2025-6 on AI transcription tools
summary: NYC Bar Association issued binding ethics guidance on lawyer use of AI transcription and note-taking tools, covering confidentiality, client consent, and supervision duties.
rationale: Major bar association codifies AI-specific duty of care for lawyers; extends professional standards into AI use.

title: FTC sets aside Rytr consent order
summary: FTC formally set aside the Rytr consent order on December 22 2025, reversing one of the flagship Operation AI Comply cases and signaling softer deceptive-AI enforcement.
rationale: Federal regulator retreating from deceptive-AI consent orders undermines consumer-protection duty-of-care layer.

title: UK AISI publishes Frontier AI Trends Report with eval data
rationale: State AISI publishes cross-lab eval results; cements expectation that evals should be public.

title: UK AISI Frontier AI Trends Report publishes cross-lab eval results
rationale: Independent eval data from state AISI reaches public; sets disclosure standard.

title: UK AISI publishes Frontier AI Trends Report with two years of model-evaluation data
summary: On 18 Dec 2025 the UK AI Security Institute released its first Frontier AI Trends Report, synthesizing two years of testing 30+ frontier models. Key findings: cyber apprentice-level task success rose from <9% (2023) to ~50% (2025); first expert-level cyber task completed in 2025; models outperform PhD-level experts on chem/bio knowledge; hour-long software tasks completed >40% of the time. Shared publicly to inform the International Network's evaluation science.
rationale: Public evidence base for internationally-aligned evaluation protocols; UK continues to anchor the network's technical output.

title: ABA Task Force on Law and AI issues Year Two report
summary: ABA Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence published its Year Two report consolidating AI ethics guidance across state bars that issued AI opinions in 2025.
rationale: Professional-standards consolidation: most state bars now have AI-specific duty rules; norm is solidifying, not just emerging.

title: OpenAI publishes GPT-5.2 update system card
rationale: OpenAI maintains per-release system card practice through late 2025.

title: Trump executive order preempts state AI laws via litigation task force
summary: On 11 Dec 2025 Trump signed EO 'Ensuring a National Policy Framework for AI' creating an AI Litigation Task Force at DOJ (established by AG memo 9 Jan 2026) to challenge state AI laws and conditioning federal grants on states not enforcing them. Colorado AI Act explicitly named. Preempts subnational compute/licensing experiments that could have aligned with international norms.
rationale: Forecloses US sub-federal compute-governance that could align with international frameworks; concentrates at minimalist federal level.

title: Australia bans under-16s from major social platforms
summary: World-first law effective 10 Dec 2025 requires Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Snapchat, YouTube, Reddit, X, Threads, Twitch, Kick to take reasonable steps to remove under-16 accounts. Penalties up to A$49.5M.
rationale: Hardest minor-protection law enacted anywhere; precedent being watched globally, Slovenia/Spain/Denmark considering similar.

title: International Network of AISIs renamed to emphasize measurement, not safety
summary: At the 4-5 Dec 2025 San Diego meeting, the network (launched at Seoul Summit May 2024; formally established Nov 2024) was renamed 'International Network for Advanced AI Measurement, Evaluation and Science' — widely read as a concession to keep the US (CAISI) engaged. UK took the Network Coordinator role. Australia joined after 25 Nov 2025 AU AISI announcement ($29.9M, operating early 2026). Members: AU, CA, EU, FR, JP, KE, KR, SG, UK, US.
rationale: Eval-protocol alignment continues at working level but under diluted branding; Australia accession expands but US posture remains uncertain.

title: EU Commission fines X €120M in first DSA non-compliance decision
summary: First DSA non-compliance fine (5 Dec 2025) for deceptive "blue checkmark" design, inadequate ad transparency, and blocked researcher access to public data.
rationale: First DSA enforcement treats deceptive verification UX as a dark pattern; establishes enforcement precedent.

title: Council of EU adopts conclusions on European competitiveness in the digital decade
summary: On Dec 5, 2025 the Council published conclusions on European Competitiveness in the Digital Decade, urging open standards, interoperability and reduced vendor lock-in in cloud, AI, cybersecurity and connectivity; asks Commission to develop common criteria for sovereign cloud services ahead of the forthcoming Cloud and AI Development Act (Commission proposal due Q1 2026).
rationale: Council-level political backing for sovereign EU compute and public AI infrastructure ahead of CADA

title: AI Workforce PREPARE Act introduced in US Senate
summary: S.3339 introduced 3 Dec 2025 by Sen. Jim Banks (R-IN) with cosponsors Hassan (D-NH), Hickenlooper (D-CO), and Husted (R-OH); establishes federal AI workforce transition fund and retraining grants. Bipartisan cosponsorship signals emerging consensus.
rationale: Federal-level displacement funding mechanism; first bipartisan AI transition bill with serious cosponsors across party lines

title: NYC Comptroller audit finds weak AEDT bias law enforcement
summary: Dec 2025 audit report finds only 18 of 391 surveyed NYC employers complied with Local Law 144 automated employment decision tool bias-audit requirements; enforcement gap exposed.
rationale: Notification/audit requirement exists on paper but is effectively unenforced; erodes compliance culture

title: Character.AI bans under-18s from open-ended chat
summary: Late Nov 2025: platform blocked minors from primary chat feature after cumulative teen-suicide lawsuits. Age-assurance functionality rolled out. Critics say late and creates dependency-withdrawal risk.
rationale: Product change driven by litigation, not well-being mission; still a rare case of a platform removing its core engagement loop for minors.

title: House GOP eyes NDAA as vehicle to revive AI state-law preemption
summary: On Nov 17, 2025, House Majority Leader Scalise confirmed to Punchbowl News that House GOP leaders are exploring attaching AI preemption language to the FY26 National Defense Authorization Act. Congressional Progressive Caucus and states-rights Republicans lined up against.
rationale: Industry allies retry democratic capture via a different legislative vehicle after the 99-1 defeat

title: Anthropic publishes political even-handedness evaluation and reports Claude Sonnet 4.5 scores
summary: Anthropic published a new automated method for measuring political bias on 13 Nov 2025, reporting Claude Sonnet 4.5 at 94% even-handedness (Opus 4.1 at 95%; Gemini 2.5 Pro 97%, Grok 4 96%, GPT-5 89%, Llama 4 66%). Method open-sourced so other labs and researchers can reproduce.
rationale: Bias-eval tooling is becoming standard and comparable across frontier labs. Politics-specific, but method is transferable.

title: EU Parliament EMPL committee adopts algorithmic management directive call
summary: Employment and Social Affairs Committee MEPs voted 11 Nov 2025 (41-6-4) to urge Commission to propose binding rules on algorithmic management across all sectors, extending Platform Work Directive protections to traditional employment. Full plenary endorsed the resolution 17 Dec 2025.
rationale: Worker voice institutionalized at EU level; binding framework for surveillance/monitoring limits advances

title: Forrester: 55% of firms that replaced workers with AI regret the decision
summary: Forrester Predictions 2026 report finds majority of enterprises that cut headcount for AI report buyer's remorse; productivity gains overestimated, rehiring underway.
rationale: Data-backed industry acknowledgment that replacement narrative oversold; shifts mainstream discourse

title: OpenAI discloses 1.2M weekly ChatGPT users discuss suicide
summary: October 27 2025: OpenAI revealed 0.15% of 800M weekly ChatGPT users (~1.2M) show explicit indicators of suicidal planning or intent. Entered mainstream coverage via TechCrunch, ABC7, News9.
rationale: Platform-disclosed scale of AI-mental-health interaction moved the debate; validated concern is not niche.

title: Meta cuts ~600 roles from AI org including FAIR
summary: Meta cut ~600 roles from its AI org (FAIR, product AI, AI infrastructure) on October 22 2025 while protecting its TBD Lab superintelligence unit; post-deployment monitoring capacity reduced.
rationale: Labs cutting teams responsible for post-deployment monitoring as deployment scales up.

title: Meta sets Instagram Teen Accounts to 13+ content default
summary: Oct 14 2025: all under-18 Instagram accounts default to 13+ content filters (inspired by MPA PG-13 guidelines); parental approval required to loosen. Strong language, alcohol, risky stunts hidden. Limited Content mode available.
rationale: Default-on teen safety settings is a meaningful de-personalization step, though narrowly applied to minors only.

title: California Governor vetoes SB 7 algorithmic management bill
summary: Gov. Newsom vetoed SB 7 on 13 Oct 2025; bill would have required human review of algorithmic employment decisions and notice of automated monitoring. Significant regression in US largest-economy jurisdiction.
rationale: High-profile veto signals industry lobbying prevailing over worker protection in US; precedent for other states

title: California AB 853 delays SB 942 AI Transparency Act effective date to August 2026
summary: SB 942 (signed 2024) would have required generative AI providers serving >1M Californians to offer free AI-detection tools and mandatory provenance disclosures from 1 Jan 2026. AB 853, signed 13 Oct 2025, pushed the effective date to 2 Aug 2026 citing implementation readiness. Mixed signal: law still advances, but compliance delayed under industry pressure.
rationale: First US state AI-content provenance regime still on track, but eight-month delay reveals industry pushback power

title: UK Upper Tribunal overturns Clearview AI ruling, remits £7.5M ICO case for reconsideration
summary: UK Upper Tribunal set aside the First-tier Tribunal's decision that had blocked the ICO's £7.5M Clearview fine, ruling Clearview's face-scraping is 'behavioural monitoring' under UK GDPR regardless of foreign law-enforcement clients. Case remitted to FTT for substantive reconsideration; Clearview subsequently granted appeal to Court of Appeal Dec 2025.
rationale: Closes a major jurisdictional loophole for offshore face-recognition vendors. Live appeal; not yet final, but directionally strong.

title: EU AI Liability Directive officially withdrawn
summary: European Commission formally withdrew the AI Liability Directive proposal; withdrawal published in Official Journal October 6 2025 after February 2025 Work Programme signalled the intent.
rationale: EU abandoning harmonized AI liability framework is the single largest duty-of-care regression of 2025.

title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT parental controls with self-harm alerts
summary: Sept 29 2025: parents can link teen accounts, set quiet hours, disable voice/memory/image gen, opt out of training. OpenAI notifies parents on detected self-harm signals. Teen Safety Blueprint published Nov 2025.
rationale: Meaningful teen-safety feature ship, but reactive to Raine lawsuit and SB 243 — not a voluntary well-being priority.

title: DeepSeek V3.2-Exp ships Sparse Attention with 50%+ API price cut
summary: DeepSeek released V3.2-Exp on Sep 29, 2025 under MIT license, debuting DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) for long-context efficiency and cutting API prices 50%+ immediately. Full V3.2 shipped Dec 1, 2025 (with V3.2-Speciale reasoning variant).
rationale: Cost collapse continues; compute-access moat at the frontier eroding through architectural innovation, not just scale.

title: FTC-driven Microsoft-OpenAI MOU restructures $13B partnership
summary: In September 2025 Microsoft and OpenAI signed a non-binding MOU to restructure their nearly $13B relationship, enabling OpenAI to transition toward a for-profit entity with freedom to partner with rival cloud providers. The restructuring was driven by threat of a formal FTC merger challenge treating multi-billion-dollar exclusive licensing as an undisclosed merger.
rationale: First concrete antitrust-driven unwinding of a cloud-AI quasi-merger. Sets precedent that exclusive frontier-lab/cloud partnerships can be

title: FTC 6(b) orders to 7 companion-chatbot companies
summary: FTC issued 6(b) orders September 11 2025 to Alphabet, Character Technologies, Instagram, Meta, OpenAI, Snap, and xAI demanding data on how they measure, test, and monitor chatbot harms to minors.
rationale: First broad federal demand for post-deployment monitoring data across frontier labs; enforcement-intelligence baseline.

title: OpenAI releases gpt-oss-120b and gpt-oss-20b under Apache 2.0
summary: First OpenAI open-weight release since GPT-2. 117B and 21B total-parameter MoE reasoning models (5.1B and 3.6B active), Apache 2.0 license, near-parity with o4-mini / o3-mini on core benchmarks. Runs on single 80GB GPU / 16GB consumer hardware respectively.
rationale: Frontier-lab capitulation to open-weights norm post-DeepSeek R1; design-layer power diffusion accelerating

title: EU AI Act GPAI obligations enter into force
rationale: GPAI providers legally required to document evals and disclose model info across EU.

title: EU AI Act Article 55 GPAI serious incident reporting obligations apply
rationale: EU-wide legal duty to report serious incidents binds GPAI providers under Article 55(1)(c).

title: EU AI Act conformity assessment framework phases in for high-risk AI
rationale: Third-party conformity assessment legally required for high-risk AI systems; phased 2025-2026.

title: EU AI Act GPAI obligations enter into force with 10^25 FLOP systemic-risk threshold
summary: On 2 Aug 2025 the EU AI Act's GPAI rules took effect, codifying a 10^25 FLOP training-compute threshold for 'systemic-risk' GPAI models subject to safety, security, and transparency obligations (10^23 FLOP threshold for baseline GPAI). First binding international compute-based safety threshold in force. Full enforcement from 2 Aug 2026.
rationale: Only binding legal regime with codified compute threshold for frontier models; reference point for international threshold convergence.

title: EU GPAI Code of Practice Safety & Security chapter signed by frontier labs
rationale: Signatories commit to adversarial-testing disclosure; voluntary but near-binding for EU access.

title: SAG-AFTRA ratifies video game contract with AI consent protections
summary: Members ratified Interactive Media Agreement 10 July 2025 after 11-month strike; contract mandates informed consent and compensation for digital replicas and AI voice/performance use.
rationale: Collective bargaining forces AI deployment terms on employers; template for other sectors

title: EU GPAI Code of Practice signed by most frontier AI developers
summary: The EU GPAI Code of Practice (Transparency, Copyright, Safety & Security chapters) was published on 10 Jul 2025 and endorsed as an 'adequate voluntary tool' by the Commission and AI Board. Signatories include Amazon, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, OpenAI, Mistral, Cohere, IBM, Aleph Alpha, and others. Meta and Chinese providers declined. xAI signed only the Safety & Security chapter.
rationale: Broadest voluntary industry commitment aligned to a binding legal regime; partial coverage (Meta/China absent) limits scope.

title: US Senate strips AI state-law moratorium from OBBBA 99-1
summary: Senate voted 99-1 during vote-a-rama to strike the Blackburn/Cantwell (bipartisan) amendment removing the 10-year (later 5-year) federal preemption of state AI laws from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. Only Sen. Tillis (R-NC) voted no.
rationale: Near-unanimous defeat of industry-backed preemption; preserves plural regulatory venues against capture and protects ~1,000 pending state

title: Apple ships Private Cloud Compute for Apple Intelligence across iOS 26 ecosystem
summary: Apple's Private Cloud Compute, extending iPhone-grade cryptographic privacy guarantees (stateless compute, verifiable transparency, non-targetability, no Apple access) to server-side inference, went into production deployment across iOS 26 and Foundation Models framework in 2025.
rationale: First consumer-scale cryptographically-attested privacy boundary for AI inference. Raises the bar for the whole stack.

title: UK AI Safety Institute renamed AI Security Institute; US counterpart renamed CAISI
summary: UK AISI renamed 'AI Security Institute' on 13 Feb 2025 (announced at Munich Security Conference by Peter Kyle), shifting emphasis from safety to security/national defense. US counterpart renamed 'Center for AI Standards and Innovation' (CAISI) on 3 Jun 2025 with explicit mandate to serve industry as NIST-housed primary point of contact. Signals reframing of voluntary evals posture in both lead jurisdictions.
rationale: Both lead government evaluators rebranded away from 'safety' toward security/innovation; chills dangerous-capability info sharing.

title: BBQ fairness benchmark now reported across every major frontier lab model card
summary: By 2025-2026, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind and Meta all report BBQ (Bias Benchmark for QA) scores in every major model card, with proprietary supplemental evaluations (first-person fairness at OpenAI, paired prompts at Anthropic, FACET/HolisticBias at Meta). NAACL 2025 (FLEX) and follow-ups note BBQ scores can mask adversarial failure modes.
rationale: De-facto norm of reporting structured bias benchmarks is stable. Coverage is US-centric and English-only; real but incomplete.

title: Anthropic CEO Amodei predicts AI will eliminate 50% of entry-level white-collar jobs
summary: Dario Amodei warns of 10-20% unemployment within 1-5 years from AI; frames displacement as near-certainty rather than choice. Prominent voice pushing replacement narrative.
rationale: Leading lab CEO normalizing mass displacement framing counteracts skepticism; elite discourse trending negative

title: Trump rescinds Biden-era AI Diffusion Rule on compute export controls
summary: On 13 May 2025 BIS announced rescission of the January 2025 AI Diffusion Rule — which would have imposed a worldwide tiered licensing framework on advanced computing ICs and model weights (including a 10^26 FLOP closed-weight threshold), with compliance set for 15 May 2025. Tiered country framework dropped; replacement rule forthcoming.
rationale: US withdrew its most ambitious frontier-compute export framework; tiered licensing + model-weight controls removed before effect.

title: BIS issues guidance on Huawei Ascend chip risks and PRC AI training
summary: Alongside AI Diffusion Rule rescission (13 May 2025), BIS issued three guidance documents: on GP10 applied to PRC-origin advanced ICs (Huawei Ascend), on controls that may apply to ICs used to train Chinese AI models, and on preventing supply-chain diversion. Narrower, adversary-focused replacement posture compared to the withdrawn diffusion rule.
rationale: Residual compute-export enforcement remains but narrower and adversary-scoped; no multilateral framework replaces the tiered diffusion rule.

title: Klarna reverses AI-first strategy, rehires humans after CX quality drop
summary: CEO Siemiatkowski publicly reversed 2024 AI-replacement strategy after customer experience deteriorated; company rehiring human agents in hybrid model. Became dominant cautionary tale in AI/labor discourse.
rationale: High-profile reversal shifts discourse from inevitability to skepticism about wholesale replacement

title: EU fines Apple €500M and Meta €200M under Digital Markets Act
summary: First DMA non-compliance fines. Apple fined €500M for App Store anti-steering (Article 5(4)); Meta fined €200M for "consent or pay" model (Article 5(2)). Both given 60 days to comply or face periodic penalty payments.
rationale: DMA teeth demonstrated; interoperability/anti-gatekeeper regime now credibly applies to the AI platform layer as gatekeepers integrate AI

title: OpenAI Memory launched with EU/EEA exclusion pending AI Act compliance review
summary: On 10 Apr 2025 OpenAI rolled out ChatGPT Memory to Plus/Pro/Team/Enterprise users globally, explicitly excluding the EU, EEA, UK, Switzerland, Norway, Iceland and Liechtenstein pending AI Act / GDPR compliance review. Users in covered regions retain view/edit/delete/export controls.
rationale: Regulatory geography actually changed default data-collection behavior in the EU. Small but concrete.

title: ChatGPT Data Controls: memory dashboard, Temporary Chats, training opt-out shipped in 2025
summary: OpenAI shipped a Data Controls settings surface in 2025: view/edit/delete individual memories, Temporary Chats (auto-delete 30d, excluded from training), export all conversations, and an 'Improve the model for everyone' opt-out toggle. Visible in UI, user-invocable; business/enterprise plans excluded from training by default.
rationale: User-visible rights controls are now standard in ChatGPT UI. Gaps remain (default-on training for consumer plans, incomplete memory export).

title: Meta excludes EU from Llama 4 multimodal models via Community License
summary: Meta released Llama 4 Scout and Maverick on Apr 5, 2025 but the Llama 4 Community License Agreement denies rights to individuals domiciled in, or companies headquartered in, the EU — a continuation of the Llama 3.2-Vision carve-out applied to all multimodal Llama models.
rationale: Geographic carve-outs fragment the "open" norm; license restrictions tighten in response to EU AI Act compliance uncertainty

title: Switzerland signs Council of Europe AI Convention
summary: Federal Councillor Albert Rösti signed the Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI on behalf of Switzerland in Strasbourg on 27 Mar 2025. Federal Council confirmed intent to ratify (12 Feb 2025); legislative consultation draft due end 2026.
rationale: Expands signatory base of the only binding international AI instrument; adds non-EU European adopter.

title: OECD common reporting framework for AI incidents published
rationale: OECD framework makes incident disclosure a tracked international norm across member states.

title: CJEU Dun & Bradstreet ruling expands GDPR Article 22 automated decision rights
summary: Court of Justice EU ruled 27 Feb 2025 (C-203/22) that data subjects have right to meaningful explanation of logic in automated decisions affecting them; strengthens worker rights against opaque scoring.
rationale: Top EU court hardens automated decision-making rights; directly applicable to workplace algorithms

title: Pew: 52% of US workers worried about AI impact on their job
summary: Pew Research finds majority of workers personally concerned about AI replacing or reshaping their role; only 36% optimistic. Concern concentrated in knowledge work.
rationale: Worker-level skepticism distinct from general public; directly relevant to consent and deployment legitimacy

title: NYPD confirms no current or planned use of AI predictive policing at City Council hearing
summary: At a Feb 2025 Committee on Public Safety hearing, NYPD Deputy Commissioner Michael Gerber confirmed the department does not use and has no plans to use AI for predictive policing, following years of heat-list and Strategic Subjects List retirements in Chicago and LA.
rationale: Normative shift: largest US department on record rejecting predictive policing. Signals rather than binds.

title: International Network of AISIs joint testing exercise on multilingual evals
summary: Singapore, Japan, UK-led joint testing exercise of Mistral Large and Gemma 2 (27B) across ten languages (11-12 Feb 2025, Paris) — 130,000+ cyber prompts, 6,000+ newly translated multilingual prompts, 40 agentic cyber tasks. Demonstrated technical information-sharing on dangerous capability evaluation between AISIs. Report published Mar 2025.
rationale: Concrete multilateral info-sharing on cyber/safety evals across jurisdictions; rare working-level evidence of AISI network delivering.

title: US and UK refuse to sign Paris AI Action Summit declaration
summary: At the Paris AI Action Summit (10-11 Feb 2025), 61 countries including China, India, Japan, Canada signed the 'Statement on Inclusive and Sustainable AI.' The US and UK declined, with VP Vance warning against 'excessive regulation.' Marked retreat from prior Bletchley/Seoul consensus.
rationale: US + UK publicly broke from multilateral AI-governance consensus; erodes public expectation of coordinated cross-border norms.

title: EU AI Act Article 5 prohibitions on biometric mass surveillance enter into force
summary: From 2 Feb 2025, the EU AI Act prohibits untargeted scraping to build facial recognition databases, emotion recognition at work/school, certain biometric categorization, and (with exceptions) real-time remote biometric identification by law enforcement. Fines up to €35M or 7% of turnover.
rationale: Largest binding biometric prohibition regime to date. Partial bans with law-enforcement exceptions, but Art.5 fines are substantial.

title: Trump EO 14110 on safe AI development revoked
rationale: US federal pre-deployment testing guidance removed; shifts regulatory burden to states and EU.

title: DeepSeek R1 release reopens open-vs-closed norm debate
summary: DeepSeek released R1 on Jan 20, 2025 under MIT license at a fraction of frontier cost; Altman later acknowledged OpenAI was "on the wrong side of history" on open weights. Triggered $589B Nvidia single-day market-cap loss on Jan 27.
rationale: Open-weights momentum shifts industry norm; forces closed-model incumbents to defend secrecy publicly and cut prices within weeks.

title: FTC Section 6(b) staff report on AI partnerships and investments
summary: FTC published 6(b) staff report on Microsoft-OpenAI, Amazon-Anthropic, Google-Anthropic partnerships. Flags concentration and competition concerns; Chair Ferguson dissented from the section identifying power-concentration implications, signalling enforcement restraint under Trump administration.
rationale: Report names the concentration problem at the cloud-AI frontier but the incoming FTC chair publicly pulled back from its policy

title: New Hampshire bans warrantless police real-time biometric surveillance (HB 1688)
summary: New Hampshire HB 1688, signed by Gov. Sununu on 12 July 2024 and effective retroactively from 1 July 2024, prohibits state agency use of real-time and remote biometric identification (including facial recognition) for surveillance in public spaces except by law enforcement with a warrant. Among the clearest state-level warrant requirements on live police biometric surveillance to date.
rationale: First clear warrant-based limit on live police biometric surveillance at state level. Meaningful even with law-enforcement exception.

title: Technical Report: Frontier Capability Assessments
summary: The Frontier Model Forum published a technical report detailing emerging industry practices for conducting frontier capability assessments.
rationale: Frontier Model Forum's report on capability assessment practices demonstrates cross-lab convergence on evaluation methodologies.

title: Coded Bias featured in Inc's eight must-watch movies for Leaders in 2020
summary: Inc. magazine included the documentary 'Coded Bias,' which explores algorithmic discrimination, in its list of must-watch movies for business leaders.
rationale: Inc. magazine featured 'Coded Bias' as a must-watch for leaders, showing mainstream recognition of algorithmic discrimination.

title: Federal study confirms racial bias of many facial-recognition systems
summary: A federal study confirmed that many facial recognition systems exhibit significant racial bias, raising mainstream concerns about their expanding use.
rationale: A US federal study confirmed widespread racial bias in facial recognition systems, driving mainstream attention to algorithmic harms.

title: Facial Recognition Technology Hearing (Committee on Oversight and Reform)
summary: The US House Committee on Oversight and Reform held a hearing titled 'Facial Recognition Technology (Part 1): Its Impact on Our Civil Rights and Liberties' to debate the surveillance risks of the technology.
rationale: US House Oversight Committee held a hearing focusing on the civil rights and liberties impacts of facial recognition technology.

title: Amazon Schooled on AI Facial Technology By Turing Award Winner
summary: A Turing Award-winning AI researcher publicly criticized Amazon's facial recognition technology, adding expert weight to ongoing concerns about algorithmic bias and accuracy.
rationale: Prominent AI researchers, including a Turing Award winner, publicly criticized Amazon's facial recognition tech over algorithmic bias.

title: Artificial Intelligence Has A Problem With Gender and Racial Bias
summary: TIME magazine published a prominent article highlighting the pervasive issues of gender and racial bias in artificial intelligence systems, bringing mainstream attention to algorithmic discrimination.
rationale: TIME magazine published an article explicitly acknowledging and bringing mainstream attention to systematic gender and racial bias in AI.

title: BERT
summary: Google released the open-source code and pre-trained weights for BERT, a state-of-the-art natural language processing model.
rationale: Google open-sourced BERT, a state-of-the-art NLP model, providing open-weights access to a frontier-class system.

title: Gender Shades: Uncovering large gender and skin-type bias in commercial AI products
summary: The Gender Shades project, led by Joy Buolamwini and Timnit Gebru, revealed that commercial facial recognition systems from major tech companies exhibited significant performance disparities, performing worst on darker-skinned females.
rationale: Landmark academic investigation exposed severe gender and skin-type bias in commercial facial recognition systems.
